claim_text: MCI patients who convert to AD show a distinct structural-MRI signature in their frozen embeddings versus non-converters.
head: linear probe

site_scanner.detail: outcome only narrowly exceeds scanner; margin CI includes zero (margin +0.06 [95% CI -0.04, +0.16])

brain_age.detail: brain-age R2=0.78, MAE=2.9yr; effect retained 106% after brain-age-GAP control (Franke/Gaser standard), 104% under the stricter predicted-brain-age control

biomarker_anchor.detail: p-tau217 correlation r=+0.46 (95% CI lower +0.26, n=77) [SYNTHETIC HARNESS: calibration target, not measured plasma]

stats.provenance: SYNTHETIC HARNESS — p-tau217/GFAP are calibration targets (calibration.CAL), not measured plasma

replication.detail: held-out cohort AUC=0.72 [95% CI 0.60, 0.84] (train 0.73); n_test=62 over 1 aggregated held-out site(s)

biology_hypothesis: Survivor “MCI patients who convert to AD show a distinct structural-MRI signature in their frozen embeddings versus non-converters.” routes to a amyloid-cascade (tau-driven) mechanism, keyed on p-tau217 and amyloid positivity. Plasma p-tau217: among the strongest blood AD biomarkers. AD-vs-CU AUC ~0.93-0.98; MCI-vs-CU ~0.94. Correlation with a structural probe score is MODEST (r~0.3-0.55), not redundant. SUPPORTED. The structural probe is read as a downstream imaging correlate of that molecular process, not an independent discovery.
next_experiment: Target N: ~120 complete-case subjects with the routing biomarker.
Direction: the structural probe score should track plasma p-tau217 (expected r~0.3-0.55, modest not redundant) and be enriched in amyloid-positive subjects.
Kill criterion: if the probe score shows no p-tau217 correlation (r<0.2) on the complete-case subset, the amyloid-cascade routing is wrong.
falsification: if the probe score shows no p-tau217 correlation (r<0.2) on the complete-case subset, the amyloid-cascade routing is wrong.
If the probe score's biomarker correlation disappears after residualizing against a scanner-predicting direction, the survivor was leakage after all.
If a held-out cohort fails to reproduce the effect (drop below the promotion floor), the mechanism claim is withdrawn.
caveats: SYNTHETIC HARNESS: the p-tau217 / GFAP anchor is a CALIBRATION TARGET (drawn to sit inside a literature range in calibration.CAL), not a measured plasma value — no open cohort pairs MRI with plasma markers. The molecular anchor demonstrates the gate MECHANIC, not a real result.
p-tau217 measured on a partial subset (~45% missing in a realistic cohort) — anchor is suggestive, not confirmatory.
Verdict is provisional and conditional on the scanner/site distribution of any replication cohort.
Brain-age adjustment uses a proxy control; residual aging signal cannot be excluded.
Any biomarker anchor is complete-case only and may not generalize.
Leakage is bounded, not eliminated — see the published batch-effect audits (‘Batch Effects in Brain Foundation Model Embeddings,’ arXiv:2604.14441 (2026)).
Promotion authorizes one confirmatory experiment, not adoption of the finding.
A ‘robust enough for follow-up’ verdict is a licence to follow up, not to conclude — the referee promotes hypotheses, it does not confirm them.

The brain-age control is a proxy, not a gold standard: it is embedding-derived (this cohort's fit: R2=0.78, MAE=2.9yr), so residual generic-aging signal can survive the adjustment and masquerade as disease-specific.
The biomarker anchor likely rests on a partial complete-case subset; with realistic p-tau217 missingness the correlation is estimated on far fewer subjects than the headline cohort.
N is small (~130); at this size AUCs are noisy and a single site's idiosyncrasy can dominate — treat effect sizes as wide-interval estimates.

The leakage test audits the embeddings with the same linear-probe family it uses for the outcome, so it bounds — but cannot fully rule out — shared confounding; this is the mechanic quantified in ‘Pretrained, Frozen, Still Leaking: Auditing Cross-Encoder Attribute Transfer in EEG Foundation Models’ (arXiv:2606.09189 (2026)), which we cite rather than claim.

evidence_ledger.detail: outcome only narrowly exceeds scanner; margin CI includes zero (margin +0.06 [95% CI -0.04, +0.16])
brain-age R2=0.78, MAE=2.9yr; effect retained 106% after brain-age-GAP control (Franke/Gaser standard), 104% under the stricter predicted-brain-age control
p-tau217 correlation r=+0.46 (95% CI lower +0.26, n=77) [SYNTHETIC HARNESS: calibration target, not measured plasma]
held-out cohort AUC=0.72 [95% CI 0.60, 0.84] (train 0.73); n_test=62 over 1 aggregated held-out site(s)

boltz_targeting.note: Boltz-2 open (MIT) AlphaFold3-class complex + binding-affinity prediction — a REAL predictor, not a stand-in; requires a GPU run

repurposing.mechanism_note: c-Abl tyrosine-kinase inhibitor hypothesized to promote autophagic clearance of misfolded amyloid/tau; explored in small early-phase Alzheimer disease studies.

boltz_affinity.note: Boltz-2 open (MIT) AlphaFold3-class complex + binding-affinity prediction — a REAL predictor, not a stand-in; requires a GPU run

repurposing.mechanism_note: RXR agonist hypothesized to enhance APOE-mediated amyloid clearance; small exploratory repurposing study in Alzheimer disease (results equivocal).

boltz_affinity.note: Boltz-2 open (MIT) AlphaFold3-class complex + binding-affinity prediction — a REAL predictor, not a stand-in; requires a GPU run

biomarker_fusion.note: multimodal fusion transformer (vkola-lab/ncomms2025) predicted amyloid/tau PET positivity over the cohort — the L3 fusion box; surfaced as pathology corroboration, NOT a referee gate

translation.wet_lab_experiment: In Tanzi-style 3D human neural organoids, knock down the lead target (CRISPRi) and read out Aβ42/40 ratio + p-tau217 by MSD at 6 weeks; kill if neither moves beyond vehicle ±2SD.
translation.caveat: Decision-support only: prioritized molecular follow-ups for a promoted imaging survivor, routed via the dominant fluid biomarker — NOT a validated target, and never derived from the imaging embedding itself.

narration: The claim “MCI patients who convert to AD show a distinct structural-MRI signature in their frozen embeddings versus non-converters.” lands at “robust enough for follow-up” (80/100). The naive effect (AUC 0.74 separating MCI converters from MCI non-converters) is real on paper, but the referee's job is to ask what else could produce it. It cleared the gauntlet's promotion floor: the effect exceeds the scanner-prediction floor on subject-disjoint splits and is worth a confirmatory follow-up — as a hypothesis, not a settled result. Where it stops being true: that the scanner/site mix in any new cohort matches this one — the leakage margin is only +0.06 AUC, so a different scanner distribution could erase the effect. That the same frozen embeddings can predict scanner/site as well as outcome is documented prior art (“Batch Effects in Brain Foundation Model Embeddings,” arXiv:2604.14441 (2026)); NeuroAD Discovery Engine only runs the audit and issues the verdict.

adjudication.prosecution: The claim that synthetic contract embeddings (badged demo cohort) separate MCI converters from MCI non-converters does not survive scrutiny. The subject-disjoint leakage margin is only +0.06 AUC — the same head predicts the scanner nearly as well as the outcome, exactly the batch-effect mechanic reported in 'Batch Effects in Brain Foundation Model Embeddings' (arXiv:2604.14441 (2026)). It failed or weakened under: Site / scanner leakage (weakened), Replication split (weakened). The parsimonious reading is an acquisition/aging artifact until an orthogonal molecular anchor says otherwise.
adjudication.defense: The signal separating MCI converters from MCI non-converters is consistent with real disease biology. It withstood: Age / sex adjustment, Brain-age control, Biomarker anchor. Critically, it is anchored to a plasma p-tau217 / GFAP correlation on the complete subset (r=0.46) — molecular pathology, not pixels.
adjudication.judge_reasoning: Robustness score 80/100 places this finding at 'robust enough for follow-up'. The +0.06 AUC leakage margin is the deciding fact: the prosecution's artifact reading is the more defensible one. The finding meets the promotion floor and may proceed to the biomarker-gated biology step — as a hypothesis to test, not a conclusion to bank.

critique: A ‘robust enough for follow-up’ verdict is a licence to follow up, not to conclude — the referee promotes hypotheses, it does not confirm them.

The brain-age control is a proxy, not a gold standard: it is embedding-derived (this cohort's fit: R2=0.78, MAE=2.9yr), so residual generic-aging signal can survive the adjustment and masquerade as disease-specific.
The biomarker anchor likely rests on a partial complete-case subset; with realistic p-tau217 missingness the correlation is estimated on far fewer subjects than the headline cohort.
N is small (~130); at this size AUCs are noisy and a single site's idiosyncrasy can dominate — treat effect sizes as wide-interval estimates.

The leakage test audits the embeddings with the same linear-probe family it uses for the outcome, so it bounds — but cannot fully rule out — shared confounding; this is the mechanic quantified in ‘Pretrained, Frozen, Still Leaking: Auditing Cross-Encoder Attribute Transfer in EEG Foundation Models’ (arXiv:2606.09189 (2026)), which we cite rather than claim.
revised_caveats: Verdict is provisional and conditional on the scanner/site distribution of any replication cohort.
Brain-age adjustment uses a proxy control; residual aging signal cannot be excluded.
Any biomarker anchor is complete-case only and may not generalize.
Leakage is bounded, not eliminated — see the published batch-effect audits (‘Batch Effects in Brain Foundation Model Embeddings,’ arXiv:2604.14441 (2026)).
Promotion authorizes one confirmatory experiment, not adoption of the finding.

claude.path: deterministic referee — Claude runs only in the orchestrator; no key set
